questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空根据标志词yet（常用于疑问句和否定句）可知需用现在完成时Have you finished；第二空根据标志词ago（一小时前）可知需用一般过去时finished。现在完成时强调过去动作对现在的影响（作业已完成），一般过去时强调过去某个具体时间发生的动作（一小时前完成的）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my cousin. He is holding a book ______ cover is red.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空先行词the boy指人，在定语从句中作主语，用who；第二空先行词a book与cover构成所属关系（书的封面），用whose作定语。which指物，whose表所属'……的'。口诀：先行词是人用who/that，是物用which/that，表所属用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new bridge ______ by the end of next month.
questions.explanation: 【解析】by the end of next month（下月底之前）是将来时间状语，需用一般将来时；bridge与complete是被动关系（桥被建成），故用一般将来时被动语态will be completed。被动语态结构：be + 过去分词，注意不同时态中be的变化。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Shanghai for a meeting.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后句'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海了，人还没回来）可知敲门'不可能'是他。can't表示否定推测'不可能'；must表示肯定推测'一定'；might表示不确定'可能'。has gone to表示去了某地未回，has been to表示去过某地已回，注意区分。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you don't know the word, you can ______ in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: 【解析】look up表示'查阅（字典、资料）'，代词it必须放在look和up中间；look for表示'寻找'；look over表示'检查'。动词+副词构成的短语，代词作宾语时必须放在中间，名词可放中间或后面，如look the word up或look up the word。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The news made everyone ______. They all felt very ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】exciting（令人兴奋的）修饰事物news；excited（感到兴奋的）修饰人everyone/they。-ing形容词表示'令人……的'，主语通常是物；-ed形容词表示'感到……的'，主语通常是人。类似用法还有boring/bored, interesting/interested等。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ______ of the mountain is covered with snow all year round.
questions.explanation: 【解析】根据语境'covered with snow all year round'（终年积雪）可知应为山顶。top顶部，bottom底部，middle中部，foot山脚。做词汇题时要结合上下文逻辑和常识（海拔越高温度越低，山顶积雪）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 【解析】固定搭配be divided into表示'被分成……'。句意：我们被分成几个小组研究不同主题。put into放入，separate from与……分离，group into分组（较少用被动）。完形填空要注意固定搭配和上下文逻辑。
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were (8)______ into several groups to research different topics. My group decided to study plastic waste in our school.

At first, we didn't know where to start. Our teacher, Mr. Li, suggested we observe the cafeteria during lunch time. We were (9)______ by what we saw. Hundreds of plastic containers and forks were thrown away every day. It was really terrible.

We then interviewed some students to find out why they used so much plastic. Most of them said it was (10)______ and they didn't have time to wash reusable containers. We realized that raising awareness was important, but providing solutions was even more crucial.

After two weeks of hard work, we came up with a plan. We proposed (11)______ up a washing station and giving rewards to students who brought their own lunch boxes. To our surprise, the school agreed to try it out.

Now, the amount of plastic waste has been (12)______ by 40%. This experience taught us that small changes can make a big difference. We feel proud that we could help make our school greener.
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后文'Hundreds of plastic containers... were thrown away... It was really terrible'可知看到大量塑料垃圾被丢弃应该是感到震惊。shocked（震惊的）程度比surprised（惊讶的）更深，更符合terrible的语境。做 Cloze 要关注情感线索和程度副词。
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were (8)______ into several groups to research different topics. My group decided to study plastic waste in our school.

At first, we didn't know where to start. Our teacher, Mr. Li, suggested we observe the cafeteria during lunch time. We were (9)______ by what we saw. Hundreds of plastic containers and forks were thrown away every day. It was really terrible.

We then interviewed some students to find out why they used so much plastic. Most of them said it was (10)______ and they didn't have time to wash reusable containers. We realized that raising awareness was important, but providing solutions was even more crucial.

After two weeks of hard work, we came up with a plan. We proposed (11)______ up a washing station and giving rewards to students who brought their own lunch boxes. To our surprise, the school agreed to try it out.

Now, the amount of plastic waste has been (12)______ by 40%. This experience taught us that small changes can make a big difference. We feel proud that we could help make our school greener.
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据后半句'they didn't have time to wash reusable containers'（没时间洗可重复使用的容器）可推断学生选择塑料是因为'方便'。convenient方便的，expensive昂贵的，difficult困难的，boring无聊的。完形填空要注意因果逻辑关系。
questions.group_passage:

questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 【解析】固定搭配set up建立、设立。句意：我们建议设立一个清洗站。put up张贴、搭建，give up放弃，build up建立（侧重逐步建立）。set up a station是常用搭配，注意积累动词短语。
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project about environmental protection. We were (8)______ into several groups to research different topics. My group decided to study plastic waste in our school.

At first, we didn't know where to start. Our teacher, Mr. Li, suggested we observe the cafeteria during lunch time. We were (9)______ by what we saw. Hundreds of plastic containers and forks were thrown away every day. It was really terrible.

We then interviewed some students to find out why they used so much plastic. Most of them said it was (10)______ and they didn't have time to wash reusable containers. We realized that raising awareness was important, but providing solutions was even more crucial.

After two weeks of hard work, we came up with a plan. We proposed (11)______ up a washing station and giving rewards to students who brought their own lunch boxes. To our surprise, the school agreed to try it out.

Now, the amount of plastic waste has been (12)______ by 40%. This experience taught us that small changes can make a big difference. We feel proud that we could help make our school greener.
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.explanation: 【解析】根据上文环保项目成功及by 40%可知塑料垃圾被'减少'了。reduced减少，increased增加，produced生产，saved节省。注意save和reduce的区别：save强调节约保留，reduce强调减少降低。amount... has been reduced数量被减少，被动语态。
questions.group_passage:

questions.stem: How many hours do teenagers actually spend on screens daily according to the study?
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第一段第一句'A recent study has found that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day'可知答案为C。A选项4-5小时是学生们自己估计的时间（第三段），B选项6小时是研究中提到的'可能产生问题'的界限。做细节题要准确定位原文，注意区分事实和观点。
questions.group_passage:
A recent study has found that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, TVs, and tablets. The research was conducted by a team from a famous university and surveyed over 1,000 students aged 13 to 18.

Dr. Chen, the lead researcher, explained that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "We found that students who spend more than 6 hours on screens daily are more likely to have sleep problems and feel anxious," he said. However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational content and video calls with family can be beneficial.

Interestingly, the study also discovered that teenagers often underestimate their screen time. When asked to guess their daily usage, most students reported 4 to 5 hours, which was significantly less than the actual figure.

To help reduce screen time, experts suggest setting clear rules. For example, keeping devices out of bedrooms during sleep time and having screen-free family meals. Parents are also advised to model good behavior by limiting their own screen use.

The study concludes that balance is key. Technology is an important part of modern life, but finding time for outdoor activities and face-to-face conversations is equally important for teenagers' development.
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Chen's words in Paragraph 2?
options: A. All screen time is bad for teenagers
B. Teenagers should never use smartphones
C. Some screen activities can be good for students
D. Most teenagers have serious mental problems
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第二段Dr. Chen的话'However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational content and video calls with family can be beneficial'可推断出有些屏幕活动是有益的。A、B选项过于绝对（all/never），D选项文中未提及'most'和'serious'。推理题要基于原文，不能过度推断，注意转折词however后的内容常是考点。
questions.group_passage:
A recent study has found that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, TVs, and tablets. The research was conducted by a team from a famous university and surveyed over 1,000 students aged 13 to 18.

Dr. Chen, the lead researcher, explained that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "We found that students who spend more than 6 hours on screens daily are more likely to have sleep problems and feel anxious," he said. However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational content and video calls with family can be beneficial.

Interestingly, the study also discovered that teenagers often underestimate their screen time. When asked to guess their daily usage, most students reported 4 to 5 hours, which was significantly less than the actual figure.

To help reduce screen time, experts suggest setting clear rules. For example, keeping devices out of bedrooms during sleep time and having screen-free family meals. Parents are also advised to model good behavior by limiting their own screen use.

The study concludes that balance is key. Technology is an important part of modern life, but finding time for outdoor activities and face-to-face conversations is equally important for teenagers' development.
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.stem: The underlined word 'excessive' in Paragraph 2 probably means ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据上下文'affect both physical and mental health'及'more than 6 hours... sleep problems'可知'excessive'应指'过多的、过量的'。too much太多，very little很少，necessary必要的，proper适当的。猜词题可通过上下文逻辑（因果、转折）和解释说明（如后文的more than 6 hours具体解释）来判断。
questions.group_passage:
A recent study has found that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, TVs, and tablets. The research was conducted by a team from a famous university and surveyed over 1,000 students aged 13 to 18.

Dr. Chen, the lead researcher, explained that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "We found that students who spend more than 6 hours on screens daily are more likely to have sleep problems and feel anxious," he said. However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational content and video calls with family can be beneficial.

Interestingly, the study also discovered that teenagers often underestimate their screen time. When asked to guess their daily usage, most students reported 4 to 5 hours, which was significantly less than the actual figure.

To help reduce screen time, experts suggest setting clear rules. For example, keeping devices out of bedrooms during sleep time and having screen-free family meals. Parents are also advised to model good behavior by limiting their own screen use.

The study concludes that balance is key. Technology is an important part of modern life, but finding time for outdoor activities and face-to-face conversations is equally important for teenagers' development.
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Use Smartphones Wisely
B. A Study on Teenagers' Screen Time
C. The Danger of Modern Technology
D. Ways to Improve Sleep Quality
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章开篇点明'A recent study has found...'，全文围绕这项关于青少年屏幕使用时间的研究展开，包括研究发现、危害、建议等。A、D选项仅为文中细节，C选项过于片面且negative（文中也提到beneficial）。主旨题要抓首段和尾段，寻找反复出现的关键词（screen time/study）。
questions.group_passage:
A recent study has found that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours and 22 minutes on screens every day. This includes time spent on smartphones, computers, TVs, and tablets. The research was conducted by a team from a famous university and surveyed over 1,000 students aged 13 to 18.

Dr. Chen, the lead researcher, explained that excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health. "We found that students who spend more than 6 hours on screens daily are more likely to have sleep problems and feel anxious," he said. However, not all screen time is harmful. Educational content and video calls with family can be beneficial.

Interestingly, the study also discovered that teenagers often underestimate their screen time. When asked to guess their daily usage, most students reported 4 to 5 hours, which was significantly less than the actual figure.

To help reduce screen time, experts suggest setting clear rules. For example, keeping devices out of bedrooms during sleep time and having screen-free family meals. Parents are also advised to model good behavior by limiting their own screen use.

The study concludes that balance is key. Technology is an important part of modern life, but finding time for outdoor activities and face-to-face conversations is equally important for teenagers' development.
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

options: A. However, this is bad for your health and grades.
B. In addition, try to keep a positive attitude towards life.
C. For example, walking to school counts as good exercise.
D. This means eating different kinds of food in proper amounts.
E. On the other hand, watching TV all day is not a good idea.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句子填空。前句提到'eat a balanced diet'（均衡饮食），D选项'这意味着吃不同种类的食物，分量适中'正好解释什么是balanced diet。注意指代关系this means...用于解释说明前文概念。
questions.group_passage:
Keeping healthy is important for everyone, especially for students. Here are some useful tips for you.

First, eat a balanced diet. (17)______ Try to eat more fruits and vegetables instead of junk food. Remember that breakfast is the most important meal of the day.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do intense workouts. (18)______ Walking, cycling, or playing ball games for 30 minutes a day is enough to keep you energetic.

Third, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to finish homework or play games. (19)______ Teenagers need at least 8 hours of sleep every night to stay focused in class.

(20)______ Learn to manage your stress by talking to friends, listening to music, or doing meditation. A healthy mind is just as important as a healthy body.
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, this is bad for your health and grades.
B. In addition, try to keep a positive attitude towards life.
C. For example, walking to school counts as good exercise.
D. This means eating different kinds of food in proper amounts.
E. On the other hand, watching TV all day is not a good idea.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前句说'不需要做高强度锻炼'，C选项'例如，走路去上学就算好的锻炼'举例说明简单运动即可。For example用于举例论证，注意与后文walking cycling等形成呼应。
questions.group_passage:
Keeping healthy is important for everyone, especially for students. Here are some useful tips for you.

First, eat a balanced diet. (17)______ Try to eat more fruits and vegetables instead of junk food. Remember that breakfast is the most important meal of the day.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do intense workouts. (18)______ Walking, cycling, or playing ball games for 30 minutes a day is enough to keep you energetic.

Third, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to finish homework or play games. (19)______ Teenagers need at least 8 hours of sleep every night to stay focused in class.

(20)______ Learn to manage your stress by talking to friends, listening to music, or doing meditation. A healthy mind is just as important as a healthy body.
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, this is bad for your health and grades.
B. In addition, try to keep a positive attitude towards life.
C. For example, walking to school counts as good exercise.
D. This means eating different kinds of food in proper amounts.
E. On the other hand, watching TV all day is not a good idea.
questions.explanation: 【解析】前句提到'stay up late'（熬夜），A选项'然而，这对你的健康和成绩都有害'承接上文指出危害。However表转折，this指代前文的stay up late。注意段落内逻辑：现象（熬夜）→评价（有害）→解决方案（需要8小时睡眠）。
questions.group_passage:
Keeping healthy is important for everyone, especially for students. Here are some useful tips for you.

First, eat a balanced diet. (17)______ Try to eat more fruits and vegetables instead of junk food. Remember that breakfast is the most important meal of the day.

Second, exercise regularly. You don't need to do intense workouts. (18)______ Walking, cycling, or playing ball games for 30 minutes a day is enough to keep you energetic.

Third, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to finish homework or play games. (19)______ Teenagers need at least 8 hours of sleep every night to stay focused in class.

(20)______ Learn to manage your stress by talking to friends, listening to music, or doing meditation. A healthy mind is just as important as a healthy body.
questions.label: 短文填空

options: A. However, this is bad for your health and grades.
B. In addition, try to keep a positive attitude towards life.
C. For example, walking to school counts as good exercise.
D. This means eating different kinds of food in proper amounts.
E. On the other hand, watching TV all day is not a good idea.
questions.explanation: 【解析】此处位于段首，是第四条建议。根据后文'manage your stress'和'healthy mind'可知讲的是心理健康。B选项'此外，尽量保持对生活的积极态度'引出心理层面建议。In addition用于补充添加并列要点，注意与First/Second/Third的并列关系，且结尾'A healthy mind'呼应'positive attitude'。
